zh.lifeSummary: 當Emma de Dinan於大約1040年出生在法國布列塔尼o-達阿爾莫迪南時，她的父親Joscelin de Dinan29歲，母親Orguen Orio de Leon23歲。她於大約1079年與Robert de Gernon III在英國英格蘭艾可斯坦斯泰德 莫特費其特結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1106年享年67歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Dinan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (now mainly counties Clare and Cork): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Daghnáin ‘descendant of Daghnán’, possibly a diminutive of dagh ‘good’.
French: habitational name from Dinan, in Côtes-d'Armor, Brittany. This surname is very rare in France.

variants.name: Dineen
meanings: Irish (Cork and Limerick): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Duinnín ‘descendant of Duinnín’, a byname from a diminutive of donn ‘brown-haired man’ or ‘chieftain’.

nameMeanings.name: Emma
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, originally a short form of compound names such as Ermintrude containing the word erm(en), irm(en) ‘entire’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, but its popularity in medieval England was greatly enhanced by the fact that it had been borne by the mother of Edward the Confessor, herself a Norman. In modern times, it was only in moderate use early in the 20th century but rose sharply in favour in the 1970s and has since remained perennially popular.

en.lifeSummary: When Emma de Dinan was born about 1040, in Dinan, Côtes-d'Armor, Brittany, France, her father, Joscelin de Dinan, was 29 and her mother, Orguen Orio de Leon, was 23. She married Robert de Gernon III about 1079, in Stansted Mountfitchet, Essex, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died in 1106, at the age of 67.

Elle a épousé Robert de Gernon III environ 1079, à Stansted Mountfitchet, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée en 1106, à l’âge de 67 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Emma de Dinanが約1040年にフランスブレタグンクoテス-ド'アルモーディナンで生まれた時，父Joscelin de Dinanは29歳，母Orguen Orio de Leonは23歳であった。約1079年にイギリスイギリスエセックススタンステド モウントフィトチェトでRobert de Gernon IIIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女は1106年に67歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Emma de Dinan(이)가 대략 1040년 프랑스 브레타그니 co츠-드'아모르 딘아에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Joscelin de Dinan(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Orguen Orio de Leon(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 대략 1079년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 스탠스테드 모우트핏쳇에서 Robert de Gernon III(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1106년 67세의 나이로 사망했다.
